mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.5% show a positive frequency below 55% or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE rises by over 0.3 percentage points within a quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both trend upward, with a combined Rank IC below 0.04 over any 24-month out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side momentum that translates into short-term positive equity returns as markets price in near-term output expansion.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month increases fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns more than 50% of the time during early-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of months where CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergent trends where nonfarm payrolls increase while the unemployment rate declines confirm genuine labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a positive return frequency below 50% or a Rank IC below 0.03 over a 36-month rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth reflects underlying economic expansion that directly supports corporate earnings trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.02 across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals immediate supply-side weakness and manufacturing contraction, which triggers risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO falls by over 0.5 percent month-over-month.
